title: Fix durable-tapes frontend performance meltdown
description: The deployed /durable-tapes page becomes unusable after a few minutes because the frontend is doing excessive runtime work. Current measurements on 2026-06-23 against https://flow.deltaisland.io/durable-tapes: raw websocket feed is modest at ~12.7 events/sec and ~25 KB/sec over 120s, but Chrome CDP measured 46.2s of post-load main-thread task time over ~174s, 38.0s script time, ~55 MB JS heap growth, and ~3,715 post-load fetch requests. A 45s URL-count probe found 456 POSTs to https://flow.deltaisland.io/lookup/options-support and 448 GETs to https://flow.deltaisland.io/option-prints/by-trace; those web-host REST URLs return 404 HTML, while the same endpoints on https://api.flow.deltaisland.io return 200 when called with the correct method. Immediate scope: route REST lookups to the API host or proxy those REST paths on flow.deltaisland.io, add backoff/negative caching so failed support hydration cannot spin, and verify /durable-tapes no longer emits repeated support lookup requests. Follow-up scope: move options support hydration/alert evidence decoration into a server-composed durable-tapes view model or batched API, and reduce route-wide React recomputation per websocket event.
acceptance_criteria: On deployed /durable-tapes, support lookup requests target a 200-serving API endpoint or are disabled when unavailable; failed lookup paths have backoff/negative cache; a 3-minute CDP probe shows no fetch storm, materially lower post-load script/task time, and no runaway heap growth; websocket data still populates the five panes correctly.
notes:
2026-06-23 native deploy follow-up: live VPS /home/delta/islandflow/.env had NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4000 and the .next bundle did not contain https://api.flow.deltaisland.io, so deployed browsers fell back to bad support REST routing. I updated only the live server env to NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.flow.deltaisland.io, rebuilt apps/web with that public env exported, restarted islandflow-web.service, and verified native web health plus rebuilt bundle api URL hit. Fresh 45s Chrome probe against https://flow.deltaisland.io/durable-tapes now targets https://api.flow.deltaisland.io, but still issues 579 /lookup/options-support and 571 /option-prints/by-trace requests, mostly aborted. CDP delta improved to TaskDuration 7.969s and ScriptDuration 7.027s over 45s, but this is still too high. CORS is healthy: OPTIONS /lookup/options-support returns 204 and POST empty lookup returns 200. Direct local API GET /option-prints/by-trace?trace_id=codex_probe timed out after 5s and API logged a Bun.serve 10s request timeout. Code read points to apps/web/features/terminal/state.tsx effects keyed to optionsFeed.items, resolved maps, visibleAlerts, and Set-valued evidence refs; live updates repeatedly invalidate effects, abort in-flight fetches, and start new ones. Next implementation should batch/debounce/dedupe support hydration by stable missing-id keys, add in-flight/negative caches, stop depending on whole mutable feed/map objects, and fix or bound the ClickHouse option-prints-by-trace miss path.
2026-06-24 Phase 00 reviewer evidence: while reviewing PR #72, bun run check:public-api-routes failed at /prints/options because https://flow.deltaisland.io returned non-JSON Cloudflare HTML. Running the same checker directly against https://api.flow.deltaisland.io failed with non-JSON OpenResty 502 HTML for /prints/options. This is outside the probe refactor but confirms deployed/public API routing remains an active verification risk for the durable-tapes performance loop.
2026-06-24 PR #72 CI evidence: Forgejo Actions Validate run #258 on d540fe6 failed at bun run scripts/check-public-api-routes.ts because /prints/options returned non-JSON text/html from https://flow.deltaisland.io. title: stabilize live api memory and reduce internal cache churn
description: The native VPS deployment is repeatedly OOM-killing islandflow-api.service during live operation. The API live cache is retaining oversized channel histories and rewriting large Redis lists on every flush, which drives multi-GB Bun RSS and heavy loopback traffic between the API, Redis, NATS, and ClickHouse. Implement an emergency VPS mitigation plus repo hardening so unsafe env values, reconnect snapshots, and Redis persistence patterns cannot push the live API back into OOM.
acceptance_criteria: 1. VPS live cache env values are reduced to safe defaults and live redis state is cleared before restart. 2. services/api/src/live.ts enforces server-side live cache caps and clamps snapshot_limit accordingly. 3. Hot generic feed Redis persistence no longer rewrites entire lists on every flush. 4. Metrics/logging expose subscription counts, snapshot sizes, redis flush volume, and API memory trend. 5. Relevant tests pass and the deployment is restarted successfully.
notes: Implemented and deployed the live-state hardening to the VPS. Final validation after restart showed the API around 120 MB RSS with capped live cache depths and clean systemd restarts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix public /replay/options proxy regression
description:
## Summary
The new deploy-time public route checker added in commit 1424a27 ("fix durable options history routing") currently fails against https://flow.deltaisland.io because GET /replay/options returns HTML instead of JSON.

## Evidence
- `bun run scripts/check-public-api-routes.ts https://flow.deltaisland.io` fails on `/replay/options?view=signal&after_ts=0&after_seq=0&limit=1` with `returned non-JSON content (text/html; charset=UTF-8)`
- `services/api/src/index.ts` implements `GET /replay/options`, so the HTML response indicates the request is landing on the web app instead of the API service
- `deployment/docker/README.md` documents that same-origin proxy mode must include `/replay/*` in the API route matcher

## Minimal Fix
Update the live reverse proxy / edge route matcher for flow.deltaisland.io so `/replay/*` is forwarded to the API host, then rerun `bun run check:public-api-routes`.

## Notes
This looks like a production proxy configuration regression rather than an in-repo application bug.

title: Plan smart-flow replacement of legacy smart-money and classifier layers
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
- The current live smart-flow channel is still projected from legacy smart-money events, while compute also emits legacy classifier hits for downstream UI/API compatibility.
- The intended domain model is smart-flow as the canonical pipeline: flow packets become evidence candidates, evidence clusters, hypothesis events, insights, alternatives, confidence, and abstention.
- We need a future migration plan that decides how to replace the legacy smart-money/classifier-hit layers without breaking existing API, UI, storage, alerting, replay, or operator workflows.

Plan the migration from legacy smart-money/classifier outputs to canonical smart-flow outputs. Define the module seam, compatibility adapters, storage/channel transition, replay/golden acceptance gates, and deletion criteria for the old path.
acceptance_criteria: A migration plan identifies smart-flow canonical compute outputs and the legacy compatibility adapters that should remain temporarily; API/UI/storage/channel consumers are inventoried with proposed transition order; replay/golden tests required before behavior flips are defined; compatibility and deletion criteria for smart-money and classifier-hits are documented; risks around alert behavior, live subscriptions, historical data, and production dashboards are called out.
